title: Occurrence Object
description: Represents an occurrence of a component at any level within a subassembly.

methods.name: activate
methods.description: Makes the occurrence the active edit target in the user interface. This is the same as enabling the radio button next to the occurrence in the browser.

methods.name: boundingBox2
methods.description: Returns the bounding box of the specified entity types within the occurrence.

methods.name: breakLink
methods.description: When the component this occurrence references is an external reference (the isReferencedComponent property returns true), this will break the link and create a local Component that this occurrence will reference. The new local Component can be accessed through the Occurrence using the component property.   This method will fail if the occurrence is not referencing an external component.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createForAssemblyContext
methods.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence. Fails if this object is not the NativeObject.

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes the occurrence from the design. If this is the last occurrence referencing a specific Component, the component is also deleted.

methods.name: getPhysicalProperties
methods.description: Returns the PhysicalProperties object that has properties for getting the area, density, mass, volume, moments, etc of this occurrence.

methods.name: moveToComponent
methods.description: Moves this occurrence from it's current component into the component owned by the specified occurrence. This occurrence and the target occurrence must be in the same context.

methods.name: replace
methods.description: Replaces this occurrence or all occurrences that reference the same external component with a new component.   This method will fail if the occurrence is not referencing an external component.

methods.name: switchConfiguration
methods.description: Changes which configuration is used for this occurrence. Use the isConfiguration property to determine if this occurrence references a configuration.

properties.name: appearance
properties.description: Read-write property that gets and sets the appearance override for this occurrence. This property can return null indicating there is no override appearance and that the contents of the occurrence are displayed using there defined appearance. Setting the property to null will remove any override appearance for this occurrence.

properties.name: asBuiltJoints
properties.description: Returns the as-built joints that affect the position of this occurrence. If the occurrence is a proxy, the as-built joints returned will also be proxies in the same context as the occurrence.

properties.name: assemblyContext
properties.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this references the component the object is defined within. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this face.

properties.name: boundingBox
properties.description: Returns the bounding box of this occurrence.

properties.name: bRepBodies
properties.description: Returns the body proxies for the B-Rep bodies in the component referenced by this occurrence. For example if you get the occurrences from the root component and then use this property to get the bodies from those occurrences, the bodies returned will return information in the context of the root component, not the component they actually exist in.

properties.name: childOccurrences
properties.description: Returns a read only list of child occurrences where only the occurrences in this occurrence's AssemblyContext are returned .

properties.name: component
properties.description: The component this occurrence references.

properties.name: configurationRow
properties.description: If this occurrence is a configuration, this property returns the row that defines it. If it isn't a configuration, null is returned.

properties.name: configuredDataFile
properties.description: Returns the DataFile object that represents the specific version of the design used by this occurrence. From the DataFile you can access other version of the file, most importantly, you can access the latest version and get the top configuration table from it.

properties.name: dataComponent
properties.description: Gets the DataComponent for the target component in the source component's document. This only applies for Occurrence's with an assembly context whose source component is a top level opened design.

properties.name: deriveFeature
properties.description: Returns the DeriveFeature if this occurrence is derived from another design. This property returns null if the occurrence is not derived from another design (i.e. isDerived property returns false).

properties.name: documentReference
properties.description: When the component this occurrence references is an external reference (the isReferencedComponent property returns true), this will return the object that represents that reference. Through the DocumentReference object you can modify the version and get other information associated with the reference.   This property will fail if the occurrence is not referencing an external component.

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the Occurrence object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same occurrence.   When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

properties.name: fullPathName
properties.description: The name of the occurrence, including the full path of occurrences as seen in the browser. The top-level component will depend on the context but will typically be the root component of the design. A name for an occurrence that is at the third level of an assembly could be "Sub1:1+Sub2:1+PartA:1".

properties.name: initialTransform
properties.description: Gets and sets the initial position of the occurrence. Setting the initial transform is not valid for all occurrences. For instance, this operation could fail if the occurrence is created by a pattern among other cases. To determine if setting the initial transform is possible, use the isValidForEditInitialPosition property. If this property returns false, attempting to set the initial transform will result in failure.

properties.name: isActive
properties.description: Gets whether this occurrence is the active edit target in the user interface. This is the same as checking the state of the radio button next to the occurrence in the browser. To activate the occurrence use the Activate method.

properties.name: isConfiguration
properties.description: Returns if this occurrence represents a configuration or not. If true, you can access the full configuration from the parent component.

properties.name: isDerived
properties.description: Returns if this occurrence is derived from another design. If true, the occurrence cannot be deleted. You should not attempt to make any edits to the component referenced by the derived occurrence. Any edits made to this derived occurrence will be lost when the derive updates.

properties.name: isGrounded
properties.description: Gets and sets whether this occurrence is pinned or not.

properties.name: isGroundToParent
properties.description: Gets and sets whether this occurrence is grounded to parent or not.

properties.name: isIsolated
properties.description: Gets and sets whether this occurrence is isolated in the UI. When an occurrence is isolated it is the only one visible in the user-interface. Only one occurrence can be isolated at a time so setting this property to true will un-isolate an occurrence that is currently isolated. Setting this property to false for an occurrence that is current isolated will un-isolate it so that no occurrence will be isolated.

properties.name: isLightBulbOn
properties.description: Gets and sets if the light bulb of this occurrence as displayed in the browser is on or off. An occurrence will only be visible if the light bulb is switched on. However, the light bulb can be on and the occurrence still invisible if a higher level occurrence in the assembly context is not visible because its light bulb is off.

properties.name: isReferencedComponent
properties.description: Gets if this occurrence is referencing an external component.

properties.name: isSelectable
properties.description: Gets and sets whether this occurrence is selectable or not.

properties.name: isVaildForEditInitialPosition
properties.description: Gets whether this occurrence is valid for edit initial position.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: Gets whether the occurrence is visible. This property is affected by the assembly context.

properties.name: joints
properties.description: Returns the joints that affect the position of this occurrence. For example, if a joint has been created between this occurrence and another occurrence, this property will return that joint. If the occurrence is a proxy, the joints returned will also be proxies in the same context as the occurrence.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the occurrence. This is the name as seen in the browser. It is a reflection of the component name with an added counter suffix (i.e. 'OccurrenceName:1').

properties.name: nativeObject
properties.description: The NativeObject is the object outside the context of an assembly. Returns null in the case where this object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object. The return type is strongly typed for each object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: orientedMinimumBoundingBox
properties.description: Returns an oriented bounding box that is best oriented to tightly fit all B-Rep bodies in the occurrence. All other geometry types are ignored.

properties.name: physicalProperties
properties.description: Returns the PhysicalProperties object that has properties for getting the area, density, mass, volume, moments, etc of this occurrence. Property values will be calculated using the 'LowCalculationAccuracy' setting when using this property to get the PhysicalProperties object. To specify a higher calculation tolerance, use the getPhysicalProperties method instead.

properties.name: preciseBoundingBox
properties.description: Returns a bounding box that tightly fits around all B-Rep bodies in the occurrence. All other geometry types are ignored.

properties.name: rigidGroups
properties.description: Returns the rigid groups that this occurrence is a member of. If the occurrence is a proxy, the joints returned will also be proxies in the same context as the occurrence.

properties.name: sourceComponent
properties.description: This is the sourceComponent for the occurrence and is affected by the assembly context. This is the top-level component where the path begins.

properties.name: timelineObject
properties.description: Returns the timeline object associated with the creation of this occurrence.

properties.name: transform
properties.description: **RETIRED** Gets and sets the 3d matrix data that defines this occurrences orientation and position in its assembly context.

properties.name: transform2
properties.description: Gets and sets the 3d matrix data that defines this occurrences orientation and position in its assembly context. This property replaces the transform property, which has been retired because there are cases where it returns the incorrect results.

properties.name: visibleOpacity
properties.description: The user can set an override opacity for components and these opacity overrides combine if children and parent components have overrides. This property returns the actual opacity that is being used to render the occurrence. To set the opacity use the opacity property of the Component object.

related_samples.name: Get Volume of Active Design API Sample
related_samples.description: Traverses through the active design and totals the volume of every body within the design.

related_samples.name: Set Vise Origin As Setup WCS Origin API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates how to define our setup WCS origin relative to our vise origin from either a component, a sketch point or a joint origin.The Work Coordinate System is a reference point set in our CAM workspace and on our machine. All machine coordinates are drawn from the WCS. This script demonstrates defining the WCS by each of 4 alternative methods:Setup by default with no origin defined.Setup using vise origin as WCS origin.Setup using a sketch point as WCS origin.Setup using a joint origin as WCS origin.

activate.title: Occurrence.activate Method
activate.description: Makes the occurrence the active edit target in the user interface. This is the same as enabling the radio button next to the occurrence in the browser.

syntax.python: returnValue = occurrence_var.activate()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = occurrence_var->activate();

activate.return_description: Returns true if the activation was successful.

boundingBox2.title: Occurrence.boundingBox2 Method
boundingBox2.description: Returns the bounding box of the specified entity types within the occurrence.

syntax.python: returnValue = occurrence_var.boundingBox2(entityTypes)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = occurrence_var->boundingBox2(entityTypes);

boundingBox2.return_description: Returns a BoundingBox3D object if the calculation was successful and null in the case where there is no valid geometry and the bounding box is empty.

parameters.name: entityTypes
parameters.description: Bitwise value that specifies the types of entities to include in the calculation of the bounding box.

breakLink.title: Occurrence.breakLink Method
breakLink.description: When the component this occurrence references is an external reference (the isReferencedComponent property returns true), this will break the link and create a local Component that this occurrence will reference. The new local Component can be accessed through the Occurrence using the component property. This method will fail if the occurrence is not referencing an external component.

syntax.python: returnValue = occurrence_var.breakLink()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = occurrence_var->breakLink();

breakLink.return_description: Returns true if the break link was successful.

related_samples.name: Break Link API Sample
related_samples.description: Iterates over all top-level occurrences and if it's a referenced component, it will break the link.

classType.title: Occurrence.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.Occurrence.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::Occurrence::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createForAssemblyContext.title: Occurrence.createForAssemblyContext Method
createForAssemblyContext.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence. Fails if this object is not the NativeObject.

syntax.python: returnValue = occurrence_var.createForAssemblyContext(occurrence)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = occurrence_var->createForAssemblyContext(occurrence);

createForAssemblyContext.return_description: Returns the proxy for the occurrence in the context of the specified occurrence.  Returns null if it failed.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that represents the context you want to create this proxy in.

deleteMe.title: Occurrence.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes the occurrence from the design. If this is the last occurrence referencing a specific Component, the component is also deleted.

syntax.python: returnValue = occurrence_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = occurrence_var->deleteMe();

deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the delete was successful.

related_samples.name: Delete Empty Components
related_samples.description: Deletes empty components from the active design.

getPhysicalProperties.title: Occurrence.getPhysicalProperties Method
getPhysicalProperties.description: Returns the PhysicalProperties object that has properties for getting the area, density, mass, volume, moments, etc of this occurrence.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = occurrence_var.getPhysicalProperties()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = occurrence_var.getPhysicalProperties(accuracy)

getPhysicalProperties.return_description: Returns a PhysicalProperties object that can be used to get the various physical property related values.

parameters.name: accuracy
parameters.description: Specifies the desired level of computational accuracy of the property calculations. The default value of 'LowCalculationAccuracy' returns results within a +/- 1% error margin.   This is an optional argument whose default value is CalculationAccuracy.LowCalculationAccuracy.

related_samples.name: Get Physical Properties API Sample
related_samples.description: Script that demonstrates getting physical properties using the API. When this script is run it will create a new document, build a simple model, and get the various physical properties from the model.

moveToComponent.title: Occurrence.moveToComponent Method
moveToComponent.description: Moves this occurrence from it's current component into the component owned by the specified occurrence. This occurrence and the target occurrence must be in the same context.

syntax.python: returnValue = occurrence_var.moveToComponent(targetOccurrence)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = occurrence_var->moveToComponent(targetOccurrence);

moveToComponent.return_description: Returns the moved Occurrence or null in the case the move failed.

parameters.name: targetOccurrence
parameters.description: The target occurrence defines both the component and the transform to apply when moving the occurrence. The occurrence will be copied into the parent component of the target occurrence and the target occurrence also defines the transform of how the occurrence will be copied so that the occurrence maintains it's same position in model space.

replace.title: Occurrence.replace Method
replace.description: Replaces this occurrence or all occurrences that reference the same external component with a new component. This method will fail if the occurrence is not referencing an external component.

syntax.python: returnValue = occurrence_var.replace(newFile, replaceAll)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = occurrence_var->replace(newFile, replaceAll);

replace.return_description: Returns true if the replacement was successful.

parameters.name: newFile
parameters.description: Specifies the DataFile you want to use as the replacement. The DataFile specified must exist in the same hub as the parent assembly.

parameters.name: replaceAll
parameters.description: Indicates if you want to replace only this single occurrence or all occurrences that reference the same external design.

switchConfiguration.title: Occurrence.switchConfiguration Method
switchConfiguration.description: Changes which configuration is used for this occurrence. Use the isConfiguration property to determine if this occurrence references a configuration.

syntax.python: returnValue = occurrence_var.switchConfiguration(newRow)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = occurrence_var->switchConfiguration(newRow);

switchConfiguration.return_description: Returns true if the switch was successful.

parameters.name: newRow
parameters.description: The row to switch to. This row must be from the same ConfigurationTopTable as the current row. You can access the table from the parent design.

appearance.title: Occurrence.appearance Property
appearance.description: Read-write property that gets and sets the appearance override for this occurrence. This property can return null indicating there is no override appearance and that the contents of the occurrence are displayed using there defined appearance. Setting the property to null will remove any override appearance for this occurrence.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = occurrence_var.appearance

# Set the value of the property.
occurrence_var.appearance = propertyValue

asBuiltJoints.title: Occurrence.asBuiltJoints Property
asBuiltJoints.description: Returns the as-built joints that affect the position of this occurrence. If the occurrence is a proxy, the as-built joints returned will also be proxies in the same context as the occurrence.

syntax.python:

assemblyContext.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this references the component the object is defined within. bRepBodies.description: Returns the body proxies for the B-Rep bodies in the component referenced by this occurrence. For example if you get the occurrences from the root component and then use this property to get the bodies from those occurrences, the bodies returned will return information in the context of the root component, not the component they actually exist in.

boundingBox.description: Returns the bounding box of this occurrence.

childOccurrences.description: Returns a read only list of child occurrences where only the occurrences in this occurrence's AssemblyContext are returned .

documentReference.title: Occurrence.documentReference Property
documentReference.description: When the component this occurrence references is an external reference (the isReferencedComponent property returns true), this will return the object that represents that reference. Through the DocumentReference object you can modify the version and get other information associated with the reference. This property will fail if the occurrence is not referencing an external component.

entityToken.title: Occurrence.entityToken Property
entityToken.description: Returns a token for the Occurrence object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same occurrence. When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = occurrence_var.entityToken

A name for an occurrence that is at the third level of an assembly could be "Sub1:1+Sub2:1+PartA:1".

For instance, this operation could fail if the occurrence is created by a pattern among other cases. propertyValue = occurrence_var.nativeObject

objectType.title: Occurrence.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = occurrence_var.objectType

orientedMinimumBoundingBox.title: Occurrence.orientedMinimumBoundingBox Property
orientedMinimumBoundingBox.description: Returns an oriented bounding box that is best oriented to tightly fit all B-Rep bodies in the occurrence. All other geometry types are ignored.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = occurrence_var.orientedMinimumBoundingBox

physicalProperties.title: Occurrence.physicalProperties Property
physicalProperties.description: Returns the PhysicalProperties object that has properties for getting the area, density, mass, volume, moments, etc of this occurrence. Property values will be calculated using the 'LowCalculationAccuracy' setting when using this property to get the PhysicalProperties object. To specify a higher calculation tolerance, use the getPhysicalProperties method instead.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = occurrence_var.physicalProperties

related_samples.name: Get Physical Properties API Sample
related_samples.description: Script that demonstrates getting physical properties using the API. When this script is run it will create a new document, build a simple model, and get the various physical properties from the model.

preciseBoundingBox.title: Occurrence.preciseBoundingBox Property
preciseBoundingBox.description: Returns a bounding box that tightly fits around all B-Rep bodies in the occurrence. All other geometry types are ignored.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = occurrence_var.preciseBoundingBox

rigidGroups.title: Occurrence.rigidGroups Property
rigidGroups.description: Returns the rigid groups that this occurrence is a member of. If the occurrence is a proxy, the joints returned will also be proxies in the same context as the occurrence.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = occurrence_var.rigidGroups

sourceComponent.title: Occurrence.sourceComponent Property
sourceComponent.description: This is the sourceComponent for the occurrence and is affected by the assembly context. This is the top-level component where the path begins.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = occurrence_var.sourceComponent

timelineObject.title: Occurrence.timelineObject Property
timelineObject.description: Returns the timeline object associated with the creation of this occurrence.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = occurrence_var.timelineObject

transform.title: Occurrence.transform Property
transform.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Gets and sets the 3d matrix data that defines this occurrences orientation and position in its assembly context. Remarks

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = occurrence_var.transform

# Set the value of the property.
occurrence_var.transform = propertyValue

transform2.title: Occurrence.transform2 Property
transform2.description: Gets and sets the 3d matrix data that defines this occurrences orientation and position in its assembly context. This property replaces the transform property, which has been retired because there are cases where it returns the incorrect results.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = occurrence_var.transform2

# Set the value of the property.
occurrence_var.transform2 = propertyValue

visibleOpacity.title: Occurrence.visibleOpacity Property
visibleOpacity.description: The user can set an override opacity for components and these opacity overrides combine if children and parent components have overrides. This property returns the actual opacity that is being used to render the occurrence. To set the opacity use the opacity property of the Component object.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = occurrence_var.visibleOpacity
